steps.title: Find the 1891 column on the graph
steps.content: The graph shows the four largest Canadian cities' populations at each of the first four national censuses (1871, 1881, 1891, 1901). The task is to compare the four bars/points at 1891.

steps.title: Compare the 1891 values
steps.content: At 1891, Montréal sits at roughly 220,000 — the tallest of the four. Toronto trails at about 180,000, with Québec City near 60,000 and Halifax around 40,000.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Toronto': second largest in 1891, not the largest — misreads which line is highest. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Montréal': highest population in 1891. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Québec City': far below Montréal and Toronto in 1891 — misreads the graph. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Halifax': the smallest of the four in 1891 — misreads the graph. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
